title: Disabling Database Referential Integrity Temporarily
text: Disabling Database Referential Integrity Temporarily When loading large amounts of data into a database that is prepared for database referential integrity, it is useful to temporarily drop referential integrity and re-create the relationship in the database. Use the Create Script facility ( /gensql ) to generate the required scripts. Before you begin, create or obtain the SQL scripts to drop referential integrity. See Generate Referential Integrity Scripts . Configure the connector to enable Uniface referential integrity by adding or removing the relevant connector option. See the connector documentation for your DBMS. Execute the scripts to drop referential integrity constraints ( *_dropReferentialIntegrity.sql ). Load the data into the tables. Run the script to validate relationship constraints ( ( *_validateReferentialIntegrity.sql ).). Investigate and resolve any reported violations of referential integrity. Execute the script to enforce referential integrity constraints ( *_createReferentialIntegrity.sql ). Related Links Generate Referential Integrity Scripts For some database systems, you can have the DBMS (instead of Uniface) manage referential integrity. To do so you need to generate referential integrity scripts and have the database administrator execute those scripts in the target database. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
